# Summary

<!-- One paragraph of what changes and why. -->

## Type of change

<!-- Pick at least one. -->

- [ ] feat (new functionality)
- [ ] fix (bug fix)
- [ ] refactor (no behaviour change)
- [ ] docs (documentation only)
- [ ] chore (build / tooling / infra)
- [ ] security
- [ ] breaking change

## Versioning & changelog

- [ ] My commits follow [Conventional Commits](https://www.conventionalcommits.org/) (`feat:`, `fix:`, `feat!:`/`BREAKING CHANGE:` for major, plus `deps:`/`ci:`/`docs:`/`chore:`).

> Do **not** hand-edit `CHANGELOG.md` — [release-please](https://github.com/googleapis/release-please) generates it and bumps the version from your commit messages on merge to `main`. The PR title should also be a Conventional Commit (squash-merge uses it).

## Test plan

<!-- Bulleted checklist of what was tested. -->

- [ ] `npm test`
- [ ] `npm run typecheck`
- [ ] Manual walkthrough of the affected surface

## Screenshots / output

<!-- If UI changed, paste before/after. If a new flow, link to the spec/plan in `docs/superpowers/`. -->

## Related

<!-- Link to spec/plan docs, related PRs, or issues. -->

- Spec: `docs/superpowers/specs/...`
- Plan: `docs/superpowers/plans/...`
